name: map_singleton_len_one
reason: Mapping a singleton set always produces a set of size 1 — this is correct but assumes the mapped result is always a single element, which is true for singletons

name: map_singleton_collapses_distinct
reason: Two distinct singleton sets map to the same result if `f` maps their elements to the same value — the lemma enables proving equality of mapped sets even when the source sets differ, potentially hiding distinctness of original elements

name: map_singleton_contains_only_image
reason: The mapped singleton contains only `f(x)` and nothing else — this is correct but callers might misuse it to conclude non-membership without checking if `b` is truly distinct from `f(x)` at the spec level

name: map_singleton_idempotent
reason: Composing maps on a singleton where `g` is a fixpoint on `f(x)` yields idempotence — this follows from the lemma but the general composition `set.map(f).map(g)` lacks a corresponding axiom for non-singletons, creating an asymmetry

name: map_singleton_subset_of_any_containing_image
reason: Any set containing `f(x)` is a superset of the mapped singleton — this is a correct consequence but could be misused to establish subset relationships between unrelated sets by routing through singleton maps
